fin.general_ledger.cap_name: General Ledger and Chart of Accounts

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper ledger book
entry.example: Hand-written entries in a physical accounts book. Common for hawkers, family-run F&B, and very small services businesses that have not yet appointed an external bookkeeper.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Single-tab Excel GL
entry.example: Excel sheet with debit / credit columns and a running balance, often emailed to an external bookkeeper monthly for review. Typical for sole proprietorships and pre-revenue SMEs.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting
established.example: Xero, QuickBooks Online, or AutoCount Cloud. Bank-feed integration auto-imports transactions; user categorises in the UI; reports run on-demand. PSG-grant-eligible for most SG SMEs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated ERP GL
integrated.example: NetSuite, Sage Intacct, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. Multi-entity consolidation, multi-currency, audit trail across all sub-ledgers. Typical for mid-market SG SMEs with international operations.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Hand-written invoices + paper receipts
entry.example: Carbon-copy invoice book or printed Word-doc invoices, with payment ticked off in a physical ledger. Cash and PayNow receipts captured on receipt slips.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel AR tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet listing invoices with paid / unpaid columns, aging buckets calculated manually. Owner sends reminder emails by hand.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud AR with bank reconciliation
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks AR module. Auto-matches PayNow / bank transfers to invoices, sends automated reminders, surfaces aging dashboard.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated AR with credit + dunning automation
integrated.example: NetSuite / Sage Intacct AR. Credit-limit checks block order entry; multi-step dunning workflows escalate to collections; DSO reports tied to sales-commission calcs.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper bill pile + manual GL posting
entry.example: Vendor invoices arrive by post or email PDF, sit in a tray, and get hand-entered into the bookkeeper's monthly summary. Common for SMEs with low vendor count + cash-led ops.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel bills tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet with vendor, bill date, amount, due date, paid flag. Owner manually flags overdue bills weekly. Common stopgap between paper and cloud accounting.

established.name: ai_extract
established.label: Cloud AP with AI invoice OCR
established.example: Cloud accounting AP (Xero, QuickBooks) paired with AI invoice OCR (Dext, Hubdoc, Veryfi). Photograph or forward a PDF; AI extracts line items, vendor, amount, GST; user reviews and posts. Often the first AI workflow SMEs adopt.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Integrated AP with PO matching
integrated.example: NetSuite / Sage Intacct AP. Three-way match between PO, goods-received, and invoice; multi-step approval routing by amount + cost-centre; vendor portal for self-service status.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper bank book + cash log
entry.example: Physical bank passbook updated weekly; cash float counted at shift end and noted in a logbook. Common for hawker stalls, small retail, single-outlet F&B.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel reconciliation sheet
entry.example: Download bank statement CSV, paste into Excel, match line items against GL transactions by date + amount. Manual but structured. Used by many SMEs pre-Xero.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting bank feed
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks / AutoCount Cloud connects directly to DBS, OCBC, UOB, Maybank, Standard Chartered SG via Plaid / Tink-equivalent feeds. Auto-imports transactions; user categorises with one-click rules.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Treasury management with multi-bank
integrated.example: NetSuite, Kyriba, or Sage Intacct treasury. Multi-bank cash pooling, FX exposure dashboards, automated payment runs via host-to-host bank integration. Typical for SG SMEs with multi-entity overseas operations.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: PDF invoices via email
entry.example: Invoices generated in Word / Excel, exported to PDF, emailed to customers. Common starting point; not InvoiceNow-compliant but still widespread among very small SMEs.

established.name: api
established.label: InvoiceNow via cloud accounting
established.example: IMDA IRSP-listed cloud accounting (Xero SG, QuickBooks SG, ABSS, AutoCount, Million). PEPPOL-compliant e-invoices sent via the access point automatically when "send via InvoiceNow" is ticked. PSG-eligible.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP-native PEPPOL access point
integrated.example: NetSuite, SAP Business One, or Sage Intacct with native PEPPOL access-point integration (often via Storecove / Tickstar providers). Multi-entity routing, custom UBL field mappings, full audit trail for IRAS GST reporting.

entry.name: manual
entry.label: Phone contacts and WhatsApp
entry.example: Customer info lives in the founder's phone contacts + WhatsApp chat history. Pricing quoted ad-hoc; no structured record of past quotes.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Customer Excel / Google Sheet
entry.example: Shared Google Sheet listing customer name, contact, last purchase. Updated by sales staff manually when they remember.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CRM (free / low tier)
established.example: HubSpot Free, Zoho CRM, or Pipedrive. Contact + activity timeline, email tracking, basic pipelines. Often the first SaaS subscription after accounting.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise CRM with hierarchies
integrated.example: Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE, or HubSpot Pro+. Account hierarchies (parent / child / division), territory management, integrated marketing automation. Also adopted by small-but-sales-led businesses (6-15 person B2B consultancies, recruitment agencies) that skip the cloud-CRM tier and go straight here because pipeline visibility is load-bearing.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Notebook + business cards
entry.example: Leads scribbled in a notebook after networking events; cards stored in a Rolodex / business card holder. Common for owner-operator services businesses, B2B trades.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Lead Excel tracker
entry.example: Excel sheet with columns for source, contact, status (cold / warm / hot), next-action date. Often shared via Google Sheets when there are 2-3 sales staff.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CRM lead pipeline
established.example: HubSpot Free, Pipedrive, Zoho CRM. Web-to-lead forms, basic lead scoring, automated email nurture sequences. Standard kit for B2B SMEs with 5+ sales staff.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Marketing automation + lead scoring
integrated.example: Salesforce + Pardot / Marketing Cloud, or HubSpot Marketing Pro+. Multi-touch attribution, predictive lead scoring, integrated SDR-to-AE handoff workflows. Typical for SaaS / B2B SMEs with dedicated marketing team.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Word-doc quotes + email orders
entry.example: Quotes drafted in Word from a template, emailed to customer, orders confirmed by email reply. Order list maintained mentally or on a paper pad. Common for trades, consultancies, one-off project work.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel quote + order book
entry.example: Excel template with line items + auto-totals; orders tracked in a separate sheet with status column. Manual handoff to accounting for invoicing.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud quoting + order tool
established.example: Xero quotes, QuickBooks estimates, QuoteWerks, or Better Proposals. Branded PDF output, quote-to-invoice conversion one-click, optional e-signature. Integrated with cloud accounting AR.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: CPQ + ERP order management
integrated.example: NetSuite Order Mgmt, Salesforce CPQ, or SAP Business One Sales. Rule-based pricing + discount approval workflows, product configurator for bundled SKUs, real-time inventory allocation on order entry.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Physical stock cards
entry.example: Pen-and-paper stock cards stuck on shelves; manual cycle counts at month-end. Typical for very small retail / F&B with low SKU count.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel SKU register
entry.example: Excel sheet with SKU, opening qty, in, out, closing qty columns. Staff updates after each delivery / sale. Reconciliation gaps are common.

established.name: barcode
established.label: POS-driven barcode inventory
established.example: POS terminal (Vend, Square, EposNow) auto-decrements stock on each sale; goods-in scanned via handheld terminal. Real-time qty across one or two outlets.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: WMS / ERP-integrated stock
integrated.example: NetSuite, SAP Business One, or a dedicated WMS (Manhattan, Blue Yonder Lite). Multi-warehouse, bin / lot / serial tracking, real-time stock across e-commerce + POS + B2B channels via API.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper SKU list / printed catalogue
entry.example: Printed price list / product catalogue used as the source of truth; updated by reprint when prices change. Common for F&B menus and small retail.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel item catalogue
entry.example: Excel sheet with SKU, name, description, cost, list price, category. Often emailed to procurement / sales for reference. Updated when new SKUs are added.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud inventory item master
established.example: Zoho Inventory, Vend, AutoCount Cloud, or Cin7 Core. SKU with variants (size / colour / unit), barcode, supplier link, re-order point. Often synced to e-commerce (Shopify, Lazada) and POS.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: PIM + ERP-integrated item master
integrated.example: NetSuite or SAP Business One item master, or a dedicated PIM (Akeneo, Pimcore). Multi-attribute schemas, multi-currency pricing, BOM relationships, where-used queries, channel-specific variants.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper PO pad / email PO
entry.example: Three-ply paper PO booklet (top copy to supplier, second to warehouse, third filed). Or a Word doc / WhatsApp message sent to supplier. Common for small trades + project work.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel PO log
entry.example: Excel sheet logging PO number, supplier, items, total, status (sent / received / paid). Manual reconciliation against supplier invoice + bank payment.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud accounting PO module
established.example: Xero / QuickBooks / AutoCount Cloud PO module. PO emailed from the system as PDF; convert PO → bill on goods-received; basic supplier history.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP P2P with vendor portals
integrated.example: NetSuite Procurement, SAP Business One P2P, or a dedicated P2P platform (Coupa, Ariba SMB). Multi-step approval routing, vendor self-service portal, blanket POs, three-way match tied to AP.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Physical HR folders
entry.example: Manila folders per employee with photocopied NRIC, work permit (if applicable), and signed offer letter. Locked in the owner's drawer; no digital backup.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: HR master Excel
entry.example: Single Excel sheet listing name, NRIC, role, start date, basic salary. Updated when hires / leavers happen. Used as the source for monthly payroll prep.

established.name: api
established.label: SG-local cloud HRIS
established.example: Talenox, HReasily, or Payboy. Employee self-service for leave / claims, integrated payroll with CPF / IRAS submissions, contract templates. Designed for SG SME statutory needs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS
integrated.example: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, or BambooHR (mid-market). Performance reviews, succession planning, learning & development modules; integrates with finance and headcount planning.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper leave forms
entry.example: Pre-printed leave application form filled in, signed by manager, filed in HR. Annual entitlement tracked manually on a wall chart or in HR's Excel.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel leave tracker
entry.example: Shared spreadsheet with employee × month grid; cells coloured for AL / MC / unpaid. Manual balance calculation. Workable for small headcounts but error-prone.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud HRIS leave module
established.example: Talenox, HReasily, Payboy, or QuickHR leave module. Employee applies via mobile app or browser; manager approves; balance auto-decrements. Statutory leave (AL / MC / parental) with SG defaults built in.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS with policy engine
integrated.example: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, BambooHR Pro. Configurable leave policies (tenure-based accruals, regional variations, carry-forward rules), integration with time / attendance and payroll, real-time absence dashboards.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Hand-calculated payroll
entry.example: Owner / bookkeeper calculates monthly pay + CPF on a calculator, writes payslip on a pre-printed slip, hands out cash or schedules bank transfer manually. Rare now, but still seen in very small family businesses.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel payroll with manual CPF
entry.example: Excel payroll template with formulas for OW / AW, employer + employee CPF, CDAC / SINDA / MBMF. Manual upload to CPF e-submission portal. Common for very small SMEs not yet on a payroll SaaS.

established.name: api
established.label: SG-local cloud payroll
established.example: Talenox, HReasily, Payboy, or QuickHR payroll. Auto-calculates CPF contributions, generates IR8A / Appendix 8A / IR21, submits CPF / IRAS electronically, integrates with SG bank GIRO payroll batches.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise HRIS payroll
integrated.example: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll, or ADP. Multi-country payroll engine, integration with finance + time + benefits, custom pay components for variable pay / commission / equity plans.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper asset list + physical tags
entry.example: Asset inventory in a binder; each asset has a hand-written number sticker. Common for small workshops, gyms, F&B with a dozen tagged items (fryers, ovens, equipment).

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel asset register
entry.example: Excel sheet with asset code, description, purchase date, cost, depreciation method, current location. Maintained by finance for tax / audit purposes; often disconnected from ops use of the assets.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud CMMS / asset management
established.example: Limble, UpKeep, Fiix, or MaintainX. Asset tree with parent / child relationships, QR-code labels for mobile scanning, work-order history per asset. Designed for SME maintenance teams.

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: EAM with RFID + IoT condition monitoring
integrated.example: IBM Maximo, SAP EAM, or Infor EAM with RFID asset tags and connected sensors (vibration, temperature, runtime hours). Predictive maintenance triggers, real-time condition dashboards. Typical for capital-intensive SG SMEs in manufacturing / logistics.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper job sheets
entry.example: Carbon-copy job sheet pad; technician keeps top copy, customer signs second, office files third. Common for trades, repair shops, small contractors.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: WhatsApp + Excel job log
entry.example: Office WhatsApps job details to technician; technician sends photo of completed job back. Office logs everything in Excel for invoicing. Surprisingly common in SG trades.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud FSM platform
established.example: Jobber, Housecall Pro, ServiceM8, or Fergus. Mobile app for technicians, GPS dispatch, quote / invoice generation, payment collection on-site. Built for sub-50-tech home services businesses.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise FSM with integrations
integrated.example: ServiceMax, Salesforce Field Service, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Field Service, or IFS FSM. Integration with CRM / ERP / inventory, complex scheduling optimisation, contract + warranty handling, customer self-service portal.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper logbook in each vehicle
entry.example: Physical logbook in each van / truck for driver to record mileage, fuel, maintenance. Common for small fleets (< 5 vehicles) — trades, food delivery, school transport.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel fleet master
entry.example: Excel sheet listing each vehicle by plate, year, model, COE expiry, road tax expiry, insurance renewal, last service date. Manual reminders / calendar entries for renewals.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud fleet management
established.example: Fleetio, AUTOsist, or Whip Around. Web + mobile app, mileage logging, maintenance scheduling, expiry alerts, fuel tracking. Some integrations with telematics dongles for basic GPS.

integrated.name: iot
integrated.label: Telematics + IoT fleet platform
integrated.example: Geotab, Samsara, Verizon Connect, or Cartrack. Real-time GPS, driver behaviour scoring (harsh braking / speeding), predictive maintenance from OBD diagnostics, integration with fuel cards + dashcams. Standard for SG logistics / delivery SMEs > 20 vehicles.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper recipe / process card
entry.example: Hand-written or printed recipe card listing ingredients / components and quantities. Used by F&B kitchens, small food manufacturers, custom job shops.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel BOM
entry.example: Excel sheet listing parent SKU, components, quantities, unit cost. Often a separate file per finished good. Hard to maintain when revisions happen.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud manufacturing BOM
established.example: Katana, MRPeasy, Cin7 Core, or Odoo Manufacturing. Multi-level BOM with auto-cost rollup from component prices, revision history, simple where-used queries. Targeted at SG SME contract manufacturers + food producers.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: ERP BOM with engineering change control
integrated.example: NetSuite Manufacturing, SAP Business One Production, or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM. Phantom BOMs, by-products, alternates, formal ECN workflow, integration with CAD / PLM for engineered products.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Sticky notes / paper task list
entry.example: Whiteboard with sticky notes per task / per person, or a paper to-do list updated daily. Common for very small creative / services teams.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Excel / Google Sheets task tracker
entry.example: Spreadsheet with task, owner, status, due date columns. Often one tab per project. Workable up to maybe 5 active projects before maintenance friction kicks in.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud project tool
established.example: Trello, Asana, ClickUp, Notion, or Monday.com. Kanban / list / Gantt views, task dependencies, file attachments, notifications. The default first-SaaS-after-accounting for many service SMEs.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: PPM / ERP project module
integrated.example: NetSuite OpenAir, Microsoft Project Online, Wrike Enterprise, or Smartsheet Advance. Resource capacity planning, portfolio dashboards, integration with timesheets / billing / finance. Typical for SG consultancies + agencies with 30+ staff billing on projects.

entry.name: paper
entry.label: Paper filing cabinet
entry.example: Physical filing cabinet with labelled folders for invoices, contracts, HR records. Common for any small business that predates the cloud era and hasn't migrated.

entry.name: spreadsheet
entry.label: Shared cloud drive (personal-tier)
entry.example: Dropbox Personal, Google Drive personal, OneDrive, or a NAS shared between staff. Folders by department / project. Works but lacks access control, retention policies, audit.

established.name: api
established.label: Cloud DMS (business tier)
established.example: Google Workspace, Microsoft 365 Business, or Dropbox Business. Per-user access, shared team drives, basic version control, mobile app. Standard kit for any SG SME with > 10 staff.

integrated.name: api
integrated.label: Enterprise DMS with retention + audit
integrated.example: SharePoint Online (with retention labels), Box, M-Files, or DocuWare. Records-management policies, version + check-in / check-out, audit trail of access, integration with ERP / CRM / HR for source-of-record linking.
